title: DOC_THINK builds a lean per-cycle tool baseline + on-demand request_tools slots (shared tool_slots.py, deny-by-default); GOAL_THINK/PM_THINK out of scope
behavior: GIVEN the documents proactive thinking cycle (apps/documents/domain.py::_think) WHEN it builds its per-cycle MCP tool surface THEN it assembles from a LEAN category baseline {core, app:documents, app:folders} + DOC's pinned custom tools (update_working_memory, save_topic_memory, mark_memories_processed) + the request_tools META tool, via the shared tool_slots.ToolSlots helper — replacing the old hardcoded tool-NAME allowlist (the needed_tools set). Categories use the registry's REAL keys (app-prefixed app:documents/app:folders — bare names resolve to ZERO tools); app:folders is required because DOC's folder tools live in a separate category from the doc tools.

GIVEN the lean baseline WHEN it is built THEN each baseline category is asserted (live, at cycle build time — assert_baseline_nonempty) to resolve INDIVIDUALLY to a non-empty tool set (an aggregate union would hide a single mis-keyed/empty category that silently degrades the surface to core-only); an empty baseline category is logged as an ERROR. A ground-truth coverage oracle (bound test) freezes DOC's old 13-tool allowlist and asserts the baseline categories' resolved-tool union is a SUPERSET of it, so no tool DOC had before is dropped.

GIVEN a new platform-root module tool_slots.py WHEN a domain uses it THEN it provides: a SLOT_CAPACITY-bounded swap-slot list sized to the domain's real common-cycle concurrency (DOC=3, not a blind inherit of chat's 2); load_slot(category) validating the category via tool_router.get_category_tool_names (so app:<id> resolves) and evicting the OLDEST non-baseline slot when full (baseline categories are never evicted); build_round_tools() = pinned + baseline ∪ loaded-slot MCP tools, minus an excluded-tool-names set, capped at the provider's 128-tool limit with truncation dropping the OLDEST slot first (a just-requested tool survives into the round it was requested for); and DYNAMIC text builders for the slot-instruction block and the available-category catalog (generated from tool_router.list_categories_text, filtered to the domain's allowlist — no hardcoded category list). tool_slots.py depends only on tool_router + mcp_client (one-directional dependency rule).

GIVEN request_tools wiring WHEN DOC_THINK runs through agent_loop.run with LoopHooks THEN a request_tools(category) call routes to slots.request_tools_response (loads/denies a slot) and flags a rebuild; after_round rebuilds the round's tools from the updated slots and injects the loaded categories' guides. An invalid category (not resolvable) loads NO slot and returns a definitive 'no such toolset' response + the dynamic catalog, so the loop never spins on a bad request.

MANDATORY DENY-BY-DEFAULT (security): DOC_THINK is a scheduled, human-out-of-the-loop cycle whose working inputs are untrusted document contents, so request_tools MUST NOT reach the whole registry (confused-deputy / prompt-injection risk). load_slot enforces a per-domain ALLOWED-CATEGORY allowlist that is deny-by-default: an EXPLICIT allowlist of DOC's safe categories (baseline {core, app:documents, app:folders} + a small reviewed read/reference set {knowledge, research, links, artifacts}), NOT 'everything minus a denylist'. A request for a category OUTSIDE the allowlist — especially a destructive/sensitive one (user management, backups/restore, credential/settings admin, messaging/email/filesystem/web, bulk-delete, any other app:<id>) — loads NO slot and returns the fail-closed 'not available to this domain' response, so a malicious document cannot self-load a destructive category. A newly-added unknown category is unreachable (fails closed). Each self-loaded category is logged (INFO), each denial logged (WARNING).

GIVEN destructive / outbound tools that ride along in the baseline categories (delete_doc, delete_folder in app:documents/app:folders; forget — deletes a memory — and broadcast_announcement, send_notification in core) WHEN the round tools are assembled THEN they are subtracted via excluded_tool_names — DOC_THINK's prior name-allowlist granted NONE of them; an unsupervised cycle over untrusted document content must ORGANIZE documents, not delete memories/docs or message users. The operator's non-negotiable 'destructive must be provably unreachable' therefore covers baseline tools, not only request_tools categories. (The coverage oracle still holds: the old 13-tool set contained none of these.)

GIVEN slots WHEN a new proactive cycle starts THEN it begins from the lean baseline with empty slots (a fresh ToolSlots per cycle; nothing persists across cycles). The prompt receives the slot-instruction block + dynamic catalog + loaded-category guides injected dynamically (no hardcoded category text baked into any .md).

OUT OF SCOPE (Gate-1 re-scope): GOAL_THINK (apps/goals/domain.py) and PM_THINK (apps/goals/pm_domain.py) — those legacy proactive paths stand down under consciousness_* flags and are deleted at Phase 5b, so lean-tooling them is wasted work; goal_work hands sessions (consciousness mid-soak, tracked in 5b reconciliation); interactive chat_domain.py (its proven inline slot logic is UNCHANGED); domain_memory.py (no LLM, no tools). The shared tool_slots.py is still built as the reusable seam for future consciousness thinking-surfaces.
implements: tool_slots.py (NEW platform-root: ToolSlots — slots + capacity, load_slot deny-by-default validating via tool_router, evict-oldest-non-baseline, build_round_tools with excluded-tool subtraction + oldest-slot-first truncation, dynamic slot-instruction + catalog text builders, assert_baseline_nonempty)

apps/documents/domain.py (_build_doc_slots replaces the hardcoded needed_tools allowlist with the lean baseline + pinned customs + request_tools; DOC_BASELINE_CATEGORIES / DOC_ALLOWED_REQUEST_CATEGORIES / DOC_EXCLUDED_TOOLS / DOC_SLOT_CAPACITY; request_tools dispatch + after_round rebuild hook; slot-instruction + dynamic catalog + guides injected into the prompt; live per-category non-empty assertion)

tests.rubric: Deterministic (stub tool_router resolver/catalog + mcp_client): load_slot resolves app:<id> categories; evicts the OLDEST non-baseline slot at capacity while baseline categories are never evicted; round tool set = baseline ∪ slots + pinned minus excluded; invalid category -> no slot + 'no such toolset' + catalog; DENY-BY-DEFAULT (out-of-allowlist sensitive/destructive category -> no slot, fail-closed); slots reset per cycle; the dynamic catalog is filtered to the allowlist (no hardcoded list); a baseline member returns 'already'.

Real registry (bootstrapped via load_all_apps): each baseline category {core, app:documents, app:folders} resolves INDIVIDUALLY non-empty; GROUND-TRUTH oracle — the baseline categories' resolved-tool union is a SUPERSET of DOC's frozen old 13-tool allowlist (no regression); DOC's pinned custom tools present; delete_doc/delete_folder present in a baseline category but excluded from DOC's surface; baseline under the 128-tool cap.

Real registry: deny-by-default filter — an allowed baseline category is not denied; a reviewed non-baseline allowed category (knowledge) LOADS; sensitive/destructive out-of-allowlist categories (skipper_email/filesystem/web/messaging) fail closed with no slot; a newly-added unknown category is unreachable; the allowlist contains NO sensitive categories (allowlist, not denylist); self-load logged INFO + denial logged WARNING.

Drive a real DOC_THINK cycle on the test host: the assembled tool surface is the lean baseline (far fewer tools than the old allowlist union, no '128 tools exceed limit' warning) with deletes excluded; a request_tools(knowledge) loads its tools + guide mid-cycle; a request_tools for a sensitive category (skipper_email) is refused fail-closed; an invalid category returns 'no such toolset' + catalog without spinning.

notes: ev-89 (operator-authored token-cost + architecture item). Gate-1 CHANGE re-scope: narrowed from {GOAL_THINK, DOC_THINK, PM_THINK} to DOC_THINK only (GOAL/PM retired under consciousness, deleted at Phase 5b; goal_work out of scope). Security fold (mandatory, from the Gate-1 review): deny-by-default per-domain allowed-category allowlist for DOC_THINK (untrusted document content + unsupervised cycle = confused-deputy risk); destructive categories provably unreachable, fail-closed, logged, bound-tested. Also folded: ground-truth coverage oracle (freeze old allowlist, assert superset — not registry-vs-registry); per-category (not aggregate) non-empty assertion; truncation drops the OLDEST slot first; SLOT_CAPACITY a stated constant + 128-cap guard. Additional security tightening (implementation): delete_doc/delete_folder ride along in app:documents/app:folders but are excluded from DOC's surface via excluded_tool_names, preserving DOC's prior no-delete boundary (the lean-category move must not silently grant deletes to an unsupervised untrusted-content cycle). Verified against the real registry: {core, app:documents, app:folders} = 12+9+10 tools and is a superset of DOC's old 13 (create_doc_in_folder is in app:folders). Architecture note (non-blocking): tool_slots.py is a shared helper with one live consumer (DOC) while chat_domain keeps parallel inline slot logic — accepted as a reusable seam; migrating chat or documenting the divergence tracked separately.
